spec_match.rationale: Exercises allowed local and tree-abiding chains after ::part(), including :hover, :focus, :focus-visible, ::before, ::selection, and ::first-line, while showing that combinators, :has(), :nth-child(), and chained ::part() remain rejected.

interactive.rationale: Toggle buttons drive a shadow-DOM custom element, a focus button moves focus into the exposed shadow part, and the selector matrix runs live CSS.supports('selector(...)') probes in the current browser.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede now promises both the newly composable pseudo chains and the structural rejection boundary, matching the live controls and selector matrix.

edge_cases.rationale: The selector boundary probe explicitly covers the still-rejected chains called out by the critique and labels unexpected support or rejection if a browser diverges.

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5195333643272192 in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: All demo colors and code samples use shared CSS variables, and every local button background override also sets color.

summary: Repaired to teach both sides of the Chrome 130 ::part() change: live styling toggles for accepted pseudo chains, a focus control for the exposed shadow part, and a selector boundary matrix that proves accepted :focus/:focus-visible/::selection/::first-line chains and rejected combinator/:has()/:nth-child()/::part() chains in the current browser.
